payload.title: Storage vs. Projection (The CQRS of Thought)
payload.description: The storage of knowledge can and must be separate from its projection. A relational database is not the information it stores; it is a search index. The underlying truth (the event stream/ledger) can have infinite, simultaneous, imperfect projections. This bridges the gap between immutable algorithmic memory (the ledger) and fluid, reconstructive human-like recall (the materialized views). All models are wrong, some are useful.
